title: Bike Touring - Netherlands & Germany - Amsterdam, Netherlands to Germany. 4-2010 First Day!

transcript.text: Hello, this is video blog number one. My second bike touring day, or my first real bike ride day. It was like 50 miles. It's been five hours and it's just, um, my zipper broke on my jacket and I had to fix that.

I rode down and I kept getting lost, um, going in circles, but I think I'm getting better. You just have to keep your eye out for all the signs, I think. Um, and it got really dark last night and I saw signs for a campsite, so I figured I'd check it out.

It was 10 Euros, but I got a, I got a shower, or I got determined it. But like, yeah, the Dutch have all kinds of animals. Check this out. Like, they have an ostrich up there. They've got a turkey right here who's been hanging out with me, ruffling his feathers all night. And over there. And then they've got deer. It's like a zoo out here.
And check out. They've also got llamas. And up in the distance, you can see that their neighbors have cows. Pretty cool. There's cows over there, too. Anyway, my neighbors, the other cameras.
So, okay. I think I'm going to get a new sleeping bag today because, uh, the one that I have is way too old and, like, the stuffing isn't even there anymore.
So, I don't have a summer cotton sleeping bag anyways. Okay, so, talk to you tomorrow. Ah, it was only two minutes.
I see I got up really late today. It's 10.30 because it was just so cold. But it's warm now and it looks like it'll be nice today. Sunny. It's been overcast since I got here. Okay.
